{"idea": {"Name": "implicit_optimization_monitoring_for_test_time_compute", "Title": "Trajectory Convergence as a Compute Controller: Grounding Test-Time Scaling in Latent Optimization Dynamics", "Short Hypothesis": "Multi-step reasoning inherently performs implicit optimization in latent space; therefore, test-time compute should be allocated based on trajectory convergence rather than heuristic token limits or output entropy. This direction is needed because current adaptive inference methods treat compute as a search problem, lacking theoretical grounding and introducing high engineering overhead. Reasoning benchmarks are the best setting to investigate this, as they exhibit clear, iterative refinement trajectories. Simpler proxies like logit entropy fail to capture multi-step structural progression, while external verifiers add prohibitive cost. Monitoring latent convergence directly tracks the underlying optimization process, providing a theoretica
